# P7.10B P7 Closeout Checklist

GeneratedUtc: 2026-05-24T19:10:19.0137128Z

This checklist captures the minimum operational criteria for closing P7 and moving into cloud deployment/live execution work.

## Automated repository checks

- PASS: SQL worker host exists â€” Dedicated SQL operational worker host project is present.
- PASS: Worker uses runnable-run discovery â€” Worker can discover SQL runs without a RunId secret.
- PASS: RunId is debug override only â€” Logs expose RunIdOverride so pinned runs are visible.
- PASS: SQL run coordinator discovery method exists â€” Coordinator exposes runnable run lookup.
- PASS: Execution-history writer exists â€” SQL execution-history writer is options-driven.
- PASS: SQL runtime bootstrap exists â€” Consolidated runtime compatibility bootstrap script is present.
- PASS: Execution-history SQL exists â€” Execution-history schema script is present.
- PASS: Work item contracts located â€” src\Core\Migration.Application\Operational\WorkItems\OperationalWorkItemQueueContracts.cs

## Required manual closeout commands

Run these from repository root before declaring P7 closed:

```powershell
powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\scripts\Validate-P710BP7CloseoutReadiness.ps1
dotnet build
dotnet user-secrets remove "SqlOperationalQueueExecutor:RunId" --project .\src\Hosts\Migration.Hosts.SqlOperationalWorker\Migration.Hosts.SqlOperationalWorker.csproj
dotnet run --project .\src\Hosts\Migration.Hosts.SqlOperationalWorker\Migration.Hosts.SqlOperationalWorker.csproj --environment Development
```

Expected local idle output when no queued runs exist:

```text
RunIdOverride=(null)
SQL operational worker host readiness check passed. Status=Ready
SQL operational queue idle. No runnable migration runs found.
```

## Local development secrets baseline

Keep only these SQL operational worker secrets locally:

```json
{
  "ConnectionStrings:MigrationOperationalStore": "Server=(localdb)\\MSSQLLocalDB;Database=MigrationOperationalStore;Trusted_Connection=True;TrustServerCertificate=True",
  "SqlOperationalQueueExecutor:Enabled": "true",
  "SqlOperationalQueueExecutor:WorkerId": "local-sql-operational-worker-01",
  "SqlOperationalQueueExecutor:BatchSize": "10",
  "SqlOperationalQueueExecutor:LeaseSeconds": "300",
  "SqlOperationalQueueExecutor:PollDelaySeconds": "5",
  "SqlOperationalQueueExecutor:RunUntilIdleAndStop": "false",
  "SqlOperationalMigrationJobExecutor:Enabled": "false"
}
```

Do not configure `SqlOperationalQueueExecutor:RunId` except as a short-lived debug override.

## P7 closeout recommendation

If the validation script passes, `dotnet build` succeeds, and the worker reaches the expected no-RunId idle state, P7 can be considered operationally closed. The next phase should focus on cloud deployment, live run creation, telemetry, and real connector execution rather than more runtime scaffolding.
